methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DroneForge is a pre-seed stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Chester Zelaya. The company has raised $2.5M to date. The relevant peer universe consists of four private companies operating in the same sector—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air—though no public comparables exist. A narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage transactions within the drone sector to meet minimum threshold requirements for statistical reliability.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed to next-stage advancement. Using the $2.5M total raised as a proxy for current implied value, this method yields $6.25M. This represents the median output of the analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than convergence across multiple independent approaches. Key uncertainties include the lack of recent transaction data for early-stage drone companies at comparable scale and the absence of revenue or operational milestones that would support revenue-multiple or DCF-based approaches. Any valuation within this range carries material risk pending additional market comparables or material company developments.
